county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Henderson. Formed 1798 from Christian. Western Kentucky Ohio River county. Henderson County Historical Society. John James Audubon State Park and museum. Henderson city records and directories from 1880s. Strong African American community — extensive enslaved persons records pre-1865. Ellis Park horse racing records. FamilySearch microfilm.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1798 to present. Continuous coverage.
Marriage bonds, licenses, and certificates from 1798. Kentucky marriage bonds are particularly valuable — they name the bride, groom, and bondsman (often a relative).

source_profiles.source_name: Henderson County Will and Estate Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Will books, estate inventories, settlements, and appraisements from 1798.

County Court order books contain guardian appointments, apprentice bindings, road orders, slave patrols, tavern licenses, and miscellaneous county business. Essential for genealogical research in Kentucky.

Tax lists naming slaveholders and number of enslaved; estate inventories naming enslaved individuals; slave schedules (1850, 1860); manumission records; bills of sale. Kentucky was a slave state that did not secede.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Tax lists and estate records generally well preserved

source_profiles.source_name: Henderson County Coal Mining and Company Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Western Kentucky coalfield. Company town records, mine payrolls, commissary records, and United Mine Workers union records. Essential for documenting mining families.
Annual tax lists enumerate heads of household, land acreage, town lots, horses, cattle, and (pre-1865) enslaved persons. Essential substitute when other records destroyed.

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in New Castle. Formed 1798 from Shelby. North-central Kentucky. Henry County Historical Society. Eminence city records. Kentucky River area. FamilySearch microfilm. Check Shelby County for pre-1798 records. Strong farming community with well-preserved agricultural census records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1798 to present. Continuous coverage.
Marriage bonds, licenses, and certificates from 1798. Kentucky marriage bonds are particularly valuable — they name the bride, groom, and bondsman (often a relative).

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Annual tax lists enumerate heads of household, land acreage, town lots, horses, cattle, and (pre-1865) enslaved persons. Essential substitute when other records destroyed.

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Clinton. Formed 1821 from Caldwell and Livingston. Far western Kentucky (Jackson Purchase region). Hickman County Historical Society. Columbus (city) Mississippi River port records. Parent county for Ballard, Carlisle, Fulton, and Graves. FamilySearch microfilm. Check Caldwell and Livingston counties for pre-1821 records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1821 to present. Continuous coverage.
Marriage bonds, licenses, and certificates from 1821. Kentucky marriage bonds are particularly valuable — they name the bride, groom, and bondsman (often a relative).

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Will books, estate inventories, settlements, and appraisements from 1821.

County Court order books contain guardian appointments, apprentice bindings, road orders, slave patrols, tavern licenses, and miscellaneous county business. Essential for genealogical research in Kentucky.

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Madisonville. Formed 1806 from Henderson. Western Kentucky. Hopkins County Historical Society and Genealogical Society. Madisonville is a regional center. Coal mining records — western KY coalfield. Dawson Springs resort heritage and health records. FamilySearch microfilm. Check Henderson County for pre-1806 records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1806 to present. Continuous coverage.
Marriage bonds, licenses, and certificates from 1806. Kentucky marriage bonds are particularly valuable — they name the bride, groom, and bondsman (often a relative).

county_metadata.parent_county: Clay, Estill, Laurel, Madison, Owsley, Rockcastle
county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created 1858 from Clay, Estill, Laurel, Madison, Owsley, Rockcastle.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in McKee. Formed 1858 from six parent counties (Clay, Estill, Laurel, Madison, Owsley, Rockcastle). Southeastern Kentucky mountains. Jackson County Historical Society. Strong Unionist area during Civil War — Home Guard records. Predominantly subsistence farming community. FamilySearch microfilm. Check all six parent counties for pre-1858 records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1858 to present. Continuous coverage.
Marriage bonds, licenses, and certificates from 1858. Kentucky marriage bonds are particularly valuable — they name the bride, groom, and bondsman (often a relative).

source_profiles.source_name: Jackson County Will and Estate Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Will books, estate inventories, settlements, and appraisements from 1858.

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Louisville. One of Kentucky's three original counties from Virginia (1780). Louisville is Kentucky's largest city and a major genealogy center. Filson Historical Society (outstanding manuscript collections, family papers). Louisville Free Public Library genealogy department. University of Louisville Archives and Records Center. Extensive African American records: enslaved persons, Freedmen's Bureau, African American newspapers. Louisville city directories from 1832. Cave Hill Cemetery records. Portland neighborhood river community records. Catholic archdiocese records (strong German, Irish communities). FamilySearch microfilm.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1780 to present. Continuous coverage.
Marriage bonds, licenses, and certificates from 1780. Kentucky marriage bonds are particularly valuable — they name the bride, groom, and bondsman (often a relative).

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Will books, estate inventories, settlements, and appraisements from 1780.

source_profiles.source_name: Virginia Land Grants — Jefferson County Area
source_profiles.repository: Library of Virginia, Richmond / KDLA, Frankfort
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Pre-statehood Virginia land grants, surveys, and patents for Kentucky District. Virginia military bounty warrants (south of Green River). Kentucky was part of Virginia until 1 June 1792.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Well preserved at LVA and KDLA

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Kentucky was a border state — both Union and Confederate service records. Union pension files at NARA; Confederate pension files at KDLA. Compiled Military Service Records for both sides. USCT (United States Colored Troops) records for African American soldiers.
Annual tax lists enumerate heads of household, land acreage, town lots, horses, cattle, and (pre-1865) enslaved persons. Essential substitute when other records destroyed.

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Nicholasville. Formed 1798 from Fayette. Central Bluegrass region. Jessamine County Historical Society. Camp Nelson National Monument (major Union supply depot and African American recruitment camp, 1863-1866) — Camp Nelson records at NARA and University of Kentucky document over 10,000 USCT soldiers and refugee families. Asbury University (1890) archives. Nicholasville city records. FamilySearch microfilm.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1798 to present. Continuous coverage.
Marriage bonds, licenses, and certificates from 1798. Kentucky marriage bonds are particularly valuable — they name the bride, groom, and bondsman (often a relative).

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Will books, estate inventories, settlements, and appraisements from 1798.

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Paintsville. Formed 1843 from Floyd, Lawrence, and Morgan. Eastern Kentucky mountains. Big Sandy River heritage. Johnson County Historical Society. Paintsville is a regional center. Mayo Mansion (John C.C. Mayo, coal baron) records. Van Lear coal mining community records (Loretta Lynn's birthplace). FamilySearch microfilm. Check parent counties for pre-1843 records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1843 to present. Continuous coverage.
Marriage bonds, licenses, and certificates from 1843. Kentucky marriage bonds are particularly valuable — they name the bride, groom, and bondsman (often a relative).

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Will books, estate inventories, settlements, and appraisements from 1843.

source_profiles.source_name: Johnson County Coal Mining and Company Records
source_profiles.repository: Southeast KY Community & Technical College / UK Special Collections
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Eastern Kentucky coalfield. Company town records, mine payrolls, commissary records, and United Mine Workers union records. Essential for documenting mining families.

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Covington. Formed 1840 from Campbell. Northern Kentucky (Greater Cincinnati metro). Kenton County Public Library Faces and Places digital collection is outstanding. Covington and Newport are major urban centers — city directories from 1860s. German and Irish immigrant heritage. Covington diocese Catholic records extensive. Behringer-Crawford Museum. FamilySearch microfilm. Check Campbell County for pre-1840 records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1840 to present. Continuous coverage.
Marriage bonds, licenses, and certificates from 1840. Kentucky marriage bonds are particularly valuable — they name the bride, groom, and bondsman (often a relative).

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created 1884 from Breathitt, Floyd, Letcher, Perry.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Hindman. Formed 1884 from Breathitt, Floyd, Letcher, and Perry. Eastern Kentucky mountains. Hindman Settlement School (1902) records — one of the earliest settlement schools in Appalachia. Knott County Historical Society. Alice Lloyd College (1923) archives. Strong oral history tradition — Appalachian oral history project recordings. FamilySearch microfilm.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1884 to present. Continuous coverage.
Marriage bonds, licenses, and certificates from 1884. Kentucky marriage bonds are particularly valuable — they name the bride, groom, and bondsman (often a relative).
